heading: Health benefits for certain former spouses
label: 4069c–1 Health benefits for certain former spouses
notes: References in TextFor the effective date of this section, referred to in subsec. (b)(1), see Effective Date note set out below.
Effective DateSection effective 90 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1988 Amendment note under .Jan. 8, 1988section 261(a) of Pub. L. 100–238section 4054 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective 90 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1988 Amendment note under .Jan. 8, 1988section 261(a) of Pub. L. 100–238section 4054 of this title

children.text: formerly married to an employee or former employee of the Foreign Service, whose marriage was dissolved by divorce or annulment before ;May 7, 1985
who, at any time during the 18-month period before the divorce or annulment became final, was covered under a health benefits plan as a member of the family of such employee or former employee; and

who was married to such employee for not less than 10 years during periods of government service by such employee, is eligible for coverage under a health benefits plan in accordance with the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Prerequisites for enrollment; notification of rights
children.label: b Prerequisites for enrollment; notification of rights
A
children.text: files an election for such enrollment; and

children.label: B
children.text: arranges to pay currently into the Employees Health Benefits Fund under an amount equal to the sum of the employee and agency contributions payable in the case of an employee enrolled under chapter 89 of such title in the same health benefits plan and with the same level of benefits.section 8909 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: to determine the identity and current address of each former spouse eligible for coverage under subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: to notify each such former spouse of that individual’s rights under this section.
The Secretary shall waive the 6-month limitation set forth in paragraph (1) in any case in which the Secretary determines that the circumstances so warrant.
Any former spouse who remarries before age 55 is not eligible to make an election under subsection (b)(1).

Any former spouse enrolled in a health benefits plan pursuant to an election under subsection (b)(1) may continue the enrollment under the conditions of eligibility which the Director of the Office of Personnel Management shall by regulation prescribe, except that any former spouse who remarries before age 55 shall not be eligible for continued enrollment under this section after the end of the 31-day period beginning on the date of remarriage.

children.heading: Prohibition on coverage by more than one plan
children.label: d Prohibition on coverage by more than one plan
children.text: No individual may be covered by a health benefits plan under this section during any period in which such individual is enrolled in a health benefits plan under any other authority, nor may any individual be covered under more than one enrollment under this section.

children.heading: “Health benefits plan” defined
children.label: e “Health benefits plan” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section the term “health benefits plan” means an approved health benefits plan under chapter 89 of title 5.
